flashcards.definition: A rock containing enough valuable mineral to be mined profitably.
Coal, oil, and natural gas formed from ancient organisms over millions of years; non-renewable energy sources.
Mineral deposit formed when hot, mineral-rich water circulates through rock and deposits minerals in fractures.
A geological structure that stops the upward migration of oil/gas, allowing it to accumulate (anticline, fault, stratigraphic).
The degree of metamorphism of coal: peat → lignite → bituminous → anthracite (increasing carbon content and energy).
